pattern: A stack keyed on indentation depth tracks the accumulated path length at each directory level so the longest file path can be read off in one linear scan.
hints: Split on newlines and measure the leading tab count to find the depth of each entry; an entry with a dot in its name is a file.

Maintain a stack where stack[d] holds the total character count of the path down to depth d (not counting the final slash); when you see a file at depth d, the answer candidate is stack[d-1] + 1 + len(name).
Push len(name) + stack[d-1] + 1 (for the slash) onto position d of the stack for directories, and update the global maximum only when the entry is a file.

edge_inputs.description: shallow two-level path with one file
deep nesting with two files, pick the longer
input:
dir
	subdir1
		file1.ext
		subsubdir1
	subdir2
		subsubdir2
			file2.ext

edge_inputs.description: directory name that contains a dot (should not count as file)
all directories, no files, must return 0
file name at maximum depth causing long path

stress.description: valid tree with many files at varying depths
stress.note: valid filesystem hierarchy with files at various depths
